q: Which phenomenon causes 'Dead Spots' in a theater?\nकिस घटना के कारण थिएटर में 'डेड स्पॉट' (जहाँ आवाज नहीं आती) बनते हैं?
e: Destructive interference of sound waves. / ध्वनि तरंगों का विनाशकारी व्यतिकरण।

q: What is 'Timbre' used for?\n'स्वरमाधुर्य' (Timbre) का उपयोग किसलिए किया जाता है?
o: To distinguish different musical instruments | विभिन्न वाद्ययंत्रों में अंतर करने के लिए
e: Quality of sound that depends on the waveform and harmonics. / ध्वनि का वह गुण जो वेवफॉर्म और हार्मोनिक्स पर निर्भर करता है।

q: The sound of 'Thunder' is heard later than the 'Flash' because:\n'बादल की गरज' 'बिजली की चमक' के बाद सुनाई देती है क्योंकि:
o: Light travels much faster than sound | प्रकाश ध्वनि से बहुत तेज चलता है
e: Light speed is $3 \times 10^8$ m/s, Sound is only $340$ m/s. / प्रकाश की गति ध्वनि से लाखों गुना अधिक है।

q: What is 'Intensity' unit in terms of Power?\nशक्ति के संदर्भ में 'तीव्रता' का मात्रक क्या है?
e: Energy per unit area per unit time. / प्रति इकाई क्षेत्र प्रति इकाई समय ऊर्जा।

q: Which part of ear contains 'Tiny Bones' (Ossicles)?\nकान के किस भाग में 'नन्हीं हड्डियाँ' (ओसिकल्स) होती हैं?
e: Includes Hammer, Anvil and Stirrup. / इसमें हैमर, एनविल और स्टिरप शामिल हैं।

q: What is 'Stirrup' (Stapes) bone specialty?\n'स्टिरप' (स्टेपीज़) हड्डी की क्या विशेषता है?
o: Smallest bone in human body | मानव शरीर की सबसे छोटी हड्डी
e: Located in the middle ear. / यह मध्य कान में स्थित होती है।

q: What is 'Acoustics'?\n'अकौस्टिक्स' (Acoustics) क्या है?
o: Study of sound | ध्वनि का अध्ययन
e: Branch of physics dealing with production and properties of sound. / भौतिकी की वह शाखा जो ध्वनि के गुणों से संबंधित है।

q: Why is 'Soft Furniture' used in sound recording studios?\nसाउंड रिकॉर्डिंग स्टूडियो में 'मुलायम फर्नीचर' क्यों उपयोग किया जाता है?
o: To absorb unwanted echoes | अवांछित गूँज को सोखने के लिए
e: Porous materials prevent sound reflection. / छिद्रपूर्ण सामग्री ध्वनि परावर्तन को रोकती है।

q: What is 'Mach 2' speed?\n'मैक 2' चाल क्या है?
o: Twice the speed of sound | ध्वनि की चाल की दोगुनी
e: Indicates supersonic speed. / यह सुपरसोनिक चाल को दर्शाता है।

q: Which factor 'Decreases' the speed of sound in air?\nहवा में ध्वनि की चाल को कौन सा कारक 'कम' करता है?
e: Speed is directly proportional to $\sqrt{T}$. Cold air means slower sound. / कम तापमान पर ध्वनि धीमी चलती है।

q: What is 'Periodic' in a wave?\nतरंग में 'आवर्ती' (Periodic) क्या होता है?
o: Motion after fixed time intervals | निश्चित समय अंतराल के बाद गति
e: Vibrations repeat themselves after a time period T. / कंपन एक समय अवधि T के बाद खुद को दोहराते हैं।

q: What is 'Sound' characterized as?\n'ध्वनि' को किस रूप में वर्णित किया जाता है?
e: Needs a medium and physical vibration to travel. / इसे यात्रा के लिए माध्यम और भौतिक कंपन चाहिए।

q: Which logic can 'Sound' not follow?\n'ध्वनि' किस नियम का पालन नहीं कर सकती?
e: Only transverse waves can be polarized; sound in air is longitudinal. / केवल अनुप्रस्थ तरंगों का ध्रुवीकरण हो सकता है।

q: What is 'Loudness' measured subjectively by?\nव्यक्तिगत रूप से 'प्रबलता' को किसके द्वारा मापा जाता है?
e: Unit of perceived loudness. / महसूस की गई प्रबलता का मात्रक।

q: What is 'Overtone'?\n'अधिस्वरक' (Overtone) क्या है?
o: Higher frequency harmonics | उच्च आवृत्ति हार्मोनिक्स
e: Frequencies higher than the fundamental frequency in a complex sound. / एक जटिल ध्वनि में मूल आवृत्ति से उच्च आवृत्तियाँ।

q: Which part of the ear is 'Snail-shaped'?\nकान का कौन सा भाग 'घोंघे के आकार' का होता है?
e: Filled with fluid and hair cells to detect sound. / ध्वनि का पता लगाने के लिए द्रव और हेयर सेल्स से भरा।

q: What is the speed of sound in 'Vacuum'?\n'निर्वात' में ध्वनि की चाल क्या है?
e: Sound cannot travel without a medium. / बिना माध्यम के ध्वनि यात्रा नहीं कर सकती।

q: The 'Pitch' of a sound depends on its:\nध्वनि की 'पिच' किस पर निर्भर करती है?
e: Higher frequency = Shrill voice, Lower frequency = Grave voice. / अधिक आवृत्ति = तीखी आवाज।

q: What is 'Shock Wave' related to?\n'शॉक वेव' किससे संबंधित है?
e: Produced by objects moving at supersonic speeds. / सुपरसोनिक गति से चलने वाली वस्तुओं द्वारा निर्मित।

q: What is the frequency of 'Stethoscope' sounds usually?\n'स्टेथोस्कोप' की ध्वनियों की आवृत्ति आमतौर पर क्या होती है?
o: Low Frequency Audible | कम आवृत्ति वाली श्रव्य
e: Focuses on heart and lung sounds which are low frequency. / हृदय और फेफड़ों की ध्वनियों पर केंद्रित।

q: Which medium conducts sound 'Slowest'?\nकौन सा माध्यम ध्वनि को 'सबसे धीमा' संचालित करता है?
e: Gases have the lowest speed of sound. / गैसों में ध्वनि की चाल सबसे कम होती है।

q: What is 'Wavelength' of a 340 Hz sound in air (v=340)?\nहवा में 340 Hz ध्वनि की 'तरंगदैर्ध्य' क्या होगी ($v=340$)?
e: Using $\lambda = v / f = 340 / 340 = 1$ m. / सूत्र $\lambda = v / f$ का उपयोग करके।

q: What is 'Infrasound' used for in nature?\nप्रकृति में 'अधोश्राव्य' (Infrasound) का उपयोग किसलिए होता है?
o: By whales to navigate | व्हेल द्वारा दिशा खोजने हेतु
e: Whales and Elephants use it for long-distance communication. / व्हेल और हाथी लंबी दूरी के संचार हेतु प्रयुक्त करते हैं।

q: What is 'Reverberation' time recommended for small classrooms?\nछोटे क्लासरूम के लिए अनुशंसित 'अनुरणन' समय क्या है?
e: To ensure clarity of speech without overlap. / ओवरलैप के बिना भाषण की स्पष्टता सुनिश्चित करने के लिए।

q: Which 'Law' explains Doppler Effect for sound?\nध्वनि के लिए डॉप्लर प्रभाव की व्याख्या कौन सा 'नियम' करता है?
e: Describes frequency shift due to relative motion. / सापेक्ष गति के कारण आवृत्ति में बदलाव का वर्णन करता है।

q: What is 'Sound' primarily?\n 'ध्वनि' मुख्य रूप से क्या है?
e: Consists of compressions and rarefactions in a medium. / माध्यम में संपीडन और विरलन शामिल हैं।

q: What is the 'Wattage' of a 2 HP motor?\n2 HP मोटर की 'वॉट क्षमता' क्या है?
e: $2 \times 746 = 1492$ Watts. / 2 गुणा 746 बराबर 1492 वाट।

q: Which polymer is used for making 'Bulletproof' vests and high-strength helmets?
e: Kevlar is a heat-resistant and strong synthetic fiber; its molecular structure makes it 5 times stronger than steel.

q: What is the 'Monomer' of Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC), used for making water pipes in Bihar?
e: PVC is made by the polymerization of Vinyl Chloride ($CH_2=CHCl$).

q: Which type of 'Plastic' can be melted and reshaped again and again?
e: Thermoplastics (like Polyethylene/PVC) soften on heating and harden on cooling.

q: What is the main difference between 'Soap' and 'Detergent' in hard water?
o: Detergent works in hard water, soap does not
e: Soaps form 'scum' with Ca and Mg ions in hard water, whereas detergents remain soluble and effective.

q: Which polymer is used to make 'Non-stick' frying pans?
e: Polytetrafluoroethylene has extremely low friction and high heat resistance.

q: What is 'Natural Rubber' chemically?
e: Natural rubber is a polymer of the monomer Isoprene ($2-methyl-1,3-butadiene$).

q: Which 'Thermosetting' plastic is used for making electrical switches and handles of pressure cookers?
e: Bakelite is a poor conductor of heat and electricity and does not melt once set.

q: What is 'Terylene' (Dacron) a type of?
e: Terylene is a popular polyester used for making clothing fibers.

q: What is the escape velocity of Earth? / पृथ्वी का पलायन वेग (Escape Velocity) क्या है?
e: It is the minimum velocity needed to leave Earth's gravity. / यह पृथ्वी के गुरुत्वाकर्षण को छोड़ने के लिए आवश्यक न्यूनतम वेग है।

q: Which layer of the Sun is visible during a total solar eclipse? / पूर्ण सूर्य ग्रहण के दौरान सूर्य की कौन सी परत दिखाई देती है?
e: Corona is the outermost layer of the Sun's atmosphere. / कोरोना सूर्य के वायुमंडल की सबसे बाहरी परत है।

q: What is the chemical name of 'Bleaching Powder'? / 'ब्लीचिंग पाउडर' का रासायनिक नाम क्या है?
e: It is used for disinfecting water and as a bleaching agent. / इसका उपयोग पानी को कीटाणुरहित करने और विरंजक के रूप में किया जाता है।

q: The 'Curry Leaf' plant belongs to which family? / 'कढ़ी पत्ता' का पौधा किस परिवार से संबंधित है?
e: It belongs to the Rutaceae family, same as citrus fruits. / यह उसी रुटासी परिवार से है जिससे खट्टे फल संबंधित हैं।

q: Which gland is called the 'Master Gland'? / किस ग्रंथि को 'मास्टर ग्रंथि' कहा जाता है?
e: Pituitary gland controls other endocrine glands. / पीयूष ग्रंथि अन्य अंतःस्रावी ग्रंथियों को नियंत्रित करती है।

q: What is the main waste product of human respiration? / मानव श्वसन का मुख्य अपशिष्ट उत्पाद क्या है?
e: Humans inhale Oxygen and exhale Carbon Dioxide. / मनुष्य ऑक्सीजन लेते हैं और कार्बन डाइऑक्साइड छोड़ते हैं।

q: What is the chemical formula of Washing Soda? / वाशिंग सोडा का रासायनिक सूत्र क्या है?
e: Sodium Carbonate Decahydrate is known as washing soda. / सोडियम कार्बोनेट डेकाहाइड्रेट को वाशिंग सोडा कहा जाता है।

q: Which gas is filled in electric bulbs? / इलेक्ट्रिक बल्बों में कौन सी गैस भरी जाती है?
e: Argon or Nitrogen is used to prevent the filament from burning. / फिलामेंट को जलने से बचाने के लिए आर्गन या नाइट्रोजन का उपयोग किया जाता है।

q: What is the S.I. unit of Power of Lens? / लेंस की क्षमता का S.I. मात्रक क्या है?
e: Power of lens is measured in Dioptre (D). / लेंस की क्षमता को डायोप्टर (D) में मापा जाता है।

q: Which metal is the best conductor of electricity? / बिजली का सबसे अच्छा सुचालक कौन सा धातु है?
e: Silver is the best conductor, followed by Copper. / चांदी सबसे अच्छा सुचालक है, उसके बाद तांबा आता है।

q: During exhalation, the diaphragm moves: / उच्छ्वास (साँस छोड़ने) के दौरान, डायाफ्राम:
e: During exhalation, the diaphragm relaxes and moves up. / साँस छोड़ने के दौरान, डायाफ्राम शिथिल होकर ऊपर की ओर बढ़ता है।

q: What is the 'Mass Defect' of a nucleus?\nनाभिक की 'द्रव्यमान क्षति' (Mass Defect) क्या है?
o: Difference between sum of nucleons and actual nuclear mass | न्यूक्लियॉन के योग और वास्तविक द्रव्यमान के बीच का अंतर
e: This missing mass is converted into binding energy according to $E=mc^2$. / यह खोया हुआ द्रव्यमान बंधन ऊर्जा में बदल जाता है।

q: Which radioactive isotope is used to study 'Plant Photosynthesis' mechanisms?\n'पौधों के प्रकाश संश्लेषण' की क्रियाविधि का अध्ययन करने के लिए किस रेडियोधर्मी समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: It helps trace the path of carbon from $CO_2$ into glucose. / यह $CO_2$ से ग्लूकोज तक कार्बन के पथ का पता लगाने में मदद करता है।

q: What is 'Nuclear Transmutation' by Alpha particles called in the Rutherford experiment?\nरदरफोर्ड के प्रयोग में अल्फा कणों द्वारा 'परमाणु तत्वान्तरण' को क्या कहा जाता है?
e: Nitrogen was converted into Oxygen by bombarding with alpha particles. / नाइट्रोजन को अल्फा कणों से टकराकर ऑक्सीजन में बदला गया था।

q: Which 'Part' of the nuclear reactor carries heat away from the core to the turbines?\nपरमाणु रिएक्टर का कौन सा 'भाग' कोर से टर्बाइन तक ऊष्मा ले जाता है?
e: Water, heavy water, or liquid sodium are commonly used. / पानी, भारी जल या तरल सोडियम का उपयोग किया जाता है।

q: What is the 'Range' of Alpha particles in air?\nहवा में अल्फा कणों की 'परास' (Range) कितनी होती है?
e: Due to their large mass and charge, they lose energy quickly by ionizing air. / बड़े द्रव्यमान के कारण वे हवा में जल्दी ऊर्जा खो देते हैं।

q: Which radiation is used in 'Brachytherapy' for internal cancer treatment?\nआंतरिक कैंसर उपचार के लिए 'ब्रेकीथेरेपी' में किस विकिरण का उपयोग किया जाता है?
o: Gamma rays from isotopes placed inside the body | शरीर के अंदर रखे गए समस्थानिकों से गामा किरणें
e: Radioactive seeds are placed directly near the tumor. / रेडियोधर्मी 'बीज' सीधे ट्यूमर के पास रखे जाते हैं।

q: What is 'Nuclear Fusion' ignition temperature approximately?\n'परमाणु संलयन' शुरू करने के लिए आवश्यक तापमान लगभग कितना है?
e: Extreme heat is needed to overcome the electrostatic repulsion of nuclei. / नाभिकों के प्रतिकर्षण को दूर करने के लिए अत्यधिक गर्मी की आवश्यकता होती है।

q: Which radioactive isotope is used in 'Industrial Radiography' to check welds in metal?\nधातु में वेल्डिंग की जांच करने के लिए 'औद्योगिक रेडियोग्राफी' में किस रेडियोधर्मी समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
o: Cobalt-60 or Iridium-192 | कोबाल्ट-60 या इरिडियम-192
e: Their high-energy gamma rays can penetrate thick steel. / उनकी गामा किरणें मोटी स्टील को भेद सकती हैं।

q: What is 'Nuclear Relaxation'?\n'परमाणु विश्रांति' (Nuclear Relaxation) क्या है?
o: Return of excited nucleus to ground state | उत्तेजित नाभिक का वापस आना
e: Energy is released as gamma radiation or heat. / ऊर्जा गामा विकिरण या ऊष्मा के रूप में मुक्त होती है।

q: Which 'Law' relates the mass defect to the binding energy?\nद्रव्यमान क्षति को बंधन ऊर्जा से कौन सा 'नियम' जोड़ता है?
o: Einstein's Mass-Energy Relation | आइंस्टीन का संबंध
e: $E = \\Delta m c^2$. / ऊर्जा = द्रव्यमान क्षति x $c^2$।

q: What is the 'Half-life' of Uranium-235?\nयूरेनियम-235 की 'अर्ध-आयु' क्या है?
e: U-235 has a shorter half-life than U-238, making it rarer in nature. / U-235 की अर्ध-आयु U-238 से कम होती है।

q: Which radioactive isotope is used to study 'Blood Circulation' and detect clots?\nरक्त परिसंचरण के अध्ययन और थक्कों का पता लगाने के लिए किस रेडियोधर्मी समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: It is injected as a saline solution to trace the blood flow. / इसे रक्त प्रवाह का पता लगाने के लिए इंजेक्ट किया जाता है।

q: What is 'Nuclear Fallout'?\n'परमाणु धूल' (Nuclear Fallout) क्या है?
o: Residual radioactive material after a nuclear blast | परमाणु विस्फोट के बाद बचा हुआ रेडियोधर्मी पदार्थ
e: It consists of dust and ash contaminated with radioactivity that falls from the sky. / यह रेडियोधर्मिता से दूषित धूल और राख है।

q: Which 'Subatomic Particle' is missing in a Protium nucleus?\nप्रोटियम नाभिक में कौन सा 'उप-परमाणु कण' अनुपस्थित होता है?
e: Protium ($^1H$) is the only atom that has no neutrons. / प्रोटियम ही एकमात्र ऐसा परमाणु है जिसमें न्यूट्रॉन नहीं होता।

q: What is the 'Decay Series' of Plutonium-239?\nप्लूटोनियम-239 की 'क्षय श्रृंखला' क्या है?
e: It follows the 4n+1 decay chain. / यह 4n+1 क्षय श्रृंखला का अनुसरण करता है।

q: Which 'Radiation' is used to change the color of gemstones (like blue Topaz)?\nरत्नों (जैसे नीले पुखराज) का रंग बदलने के लिए किस 'विकिरण' का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: High-energy radiation alters the crystal structure, changing its color. / उच्च ऊर्जा विकिरण क्रिस्टल संरचना को बदल देता है।

q: What is 'Radiolysis'?\n'रेडियोलिसिस' (Radiolysis) क्या है?
o: Decomposition of molecules by ionizing radiation | आयनकारी विकिरण द्वारा अणुओं का अपघटन
e: Example: Decomposition of water into $H_2$ and $O_2$ by radiation. / उदाहरण: विकिरण द्वारा पानी का अपघटन।

q: Which isotope is used in 'Carbon-11 PET Scan' for brain imaging?\nमस्तिष्क इमेजिंग के लिए 'कार्बन-11 PET स्कैन' में किस समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: C-11 has a very short half-life of 20 minutes, ideal for medical scans. / C-11 की अर्ध-आयु केवल 20 मिनट होती है।

q: What is 'Nuclear Magnetic Resonance' (NMR) primarily used for?\n'परमाणु चुंबकीय अनुनाद' (NMR) का मुख्य रूप से किस लिए उपयोग किया जाता है?
o: Studying molecular structure and MRI | आणविक संरचना और MRI
e: It uses the magnetic properties of certain nuclei. / यह कुछ नाभिकों के चुंबकीय गुणों का उपयोग करता है।

q: Which 'Force' overcomes the electrostatic repulsion between protons in the nucleus?\nनाभिक में प्रोटॉन के बीच स्थिरवैद्युत प्रतिकर्षण को कौन सा 'बल' नियंत्रित करता है?
o: Strong Nuclear Force | प्रबल नाभिकीय बल
e: It is the strongest force in nature but acts only at very short ranges. / यह प्रकृति का सबसे मजबूत बल है।

q: What is the 'Half-life' of Tritium (an isotope of Hydrogen)?\nट्रिटियम (हाइड्रोजन का एक समस्थानिक) की 'अर्ध-आयु' क्या है?
e: Tritium is a weak beta emitter used in self-powered lighting. / ट्रिटियम एक कमजोर बीटा उत्सर्जक है।

q: Which radioactive isotope is used in 'Pace-makers' for the heart?\nहृदय के 'पेसमेकर' में किस रेडियोधर्मी समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: It provides a long-lasting power source for the device. / यह उपकरण के लिए लंबे समय तक चलने वाला शक्ति स्रोत प्रदान करता है।

q: What is 'Nuclear Cross-section' measured in?\n'परमाणु क्रॉस-सेक्शन' (Nuclear Cross-section) किसमें मापा जाता है?
e: 1 Barn = $10^{-28} \\text{ m}^2$. It measures the probability of a nuclear reaction. / यह परमाणु अभिक्रिया की संभावना को मापता है।

q: Which type of 'Nuclear Reaction' occurs in a Hydrogen Bomb?\nहाइड्रोजन बम में किस प्रकार की 'परमाणु अभिक्रिया' होती है?
e: It is also called a thermonuclear reaction. / इसे थर्मोन्यूक्लियर अभिक्रिया भी कहा जाता है।

q: What is 'K-capture' in radioactivity?\nरेडियोधर्मिता में 'K-कैप्चर' क्या है?
o: Absorption of K-shell electron by nucleus | नाभिक द्वारा K-कोश के इलेक्ट्रॉन का अवशोषण
e: This results in the conversion of a proton into a neutron. / इसके परिणामस्वरूप एक प्रोटॉन न्यूट्रॉन में बदल जाता है।

q: Which 'Shielding Material' is best for blocking Neutron radiation?\nन्यूट्रॉन विकिरण को रोकने के लिए सबसे अच्छा 'परिरक्षण पदार्थ' क्या है?
o: Water or Paraffin | पानी या पैराफिन
e: Light atoms like Hydrogen in water slow down and absorb neutrons. / पानी में हाइड्रोजन जैसे हल्के परमाणु न्यूट्रॉन को धीमा करते हैं।

q: What is the 'Activity' of a radioactive sample?\nएक रेडियोधर्मी नमूने की 'सक्रियता' क्या है?
o: Disintegrations per unit time | प्रति इकाई समय में विखंडन की संख्या
e: Measured in Becquerel (Bq). / इसे बेकरेल (Bq) में मापा जाता है।

q: Which particle is identical to a 'Beta-minus' particle?\nकौन सा कण 'बीटा-माइनस' कण के समान है?
e: Beta-minus particles are high-speed electrons. / बीटा-माइनस कण उच्च गति वाले इलेक्ट्रॉन हैं।

q: What is 'Nuclear Waste' primarily stored in for long-term disposal?\nदीर्घकालिक निपटान के लिए 'परमाणु कचरे' को मुख्य रूप से किसमें संग्रहित किया जाता है?
o: Vitrified glass/Steel canisters | कांच या स्टील के कनस्तर
e: Waste is mixed with glass (vitrification) and sealed in deep underground vaults. / कचरे को कांच के साथ मिलाया जाता है और गहरी तिजोरियों में सील कर दिया जाता है।

q: Which radioactive isotope is used to determine the age of 'Ancient Paintings' or Manuscripts?\n'प्राचीन चित्रों' या पांडुलिपियों की आयु निर्धारित करने के लिए किस रेडियोधर्मी समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Radiocarbon dating is effective for organic materials up to 50,000 years old. / कार्बन डेटिंग 50,000 साल पुरानी जैविक सामग्री के लिए प्रभावी है।

q: Which Article provides for the 'Appointment of Retired Judges' in SC sessions? / सुप्रीम कोर्ट के सत्रों में 'सेवानिवृत्त न्यायाधीशों की नियुक्ति' का प्रावधान कौन सा अनुच्छेद करता है?
e: 1. Core Concept: Utilizing Expertise. \\n2. Logic: Agar kaam bahut zyada ho, toh CJI President ki ijazat se retired judges ko kaam par bula sakta hai. \\n3. Extra Fact: Unhe wahi suvidhayein milti hain jo ek SC judge ko milti hain.

q: What is the 'Half-life' of Radium-226 approximately?\nरेडियम-226 की अनुमानित 'अर्ध-आयु' क्या है?
e: It was used in early luminous watches. / इसका उपयोग शुरुआती चमकदार घड़ियों में किया जाता था।

q: Which particle has 'Zero' mass and 'Zero' charge?\nकिस कण का द्रव्यमान 'शून्य' और आवेश 'शून्य' होता है?
e: Neutrinos are emitted during beta decay. / बीटा क्षय के दौरान न्यूट्रिनो उत्सर्जित होते हैं।

q: What is 'Nuclear Chain Reaction' multiplication factor (k) for stability?\nस्थिरता के लिए परमाणु श्रृंखला अभिक्रिया का 'गुणन कारक' (k) क्या होना चाहिए?
e: If k = 1, the reaction is self-sustaining and controlled. / यदि k = 1 है, तो अभिक्रिया नियंत्रित रहती है।

q: Which radioactive element is extracted from 'Pitchblende' ore?\n'पिचब्लेंड' अयस्क से कौन सा रेडियोधर्मी तत्व निकाला जाता है?
o: Both B and C | B और C दोनों
e: Pitchblende is the primary source of Uranium and Radium. / पिचब्लेंड यूरेनियम और रेडियम का प्राथमिक स्रोत है।

q: What is 'Artificial Transmutation'?\n'कृत्रिम तत्वान्तरण' (Artificial Transmutation) क्या है?
o: Changing one element into another by bombardment | बमबारी द्वारा एक तत्व को दूसरे में बदलना
e: First achieved by Rutherford in 1919. / 1919 में रदरफोर्ड द्वारा पहली बार किया गया था।

q: Which rays are 'Electromagnetic' in nature?\nकौन सी किरणें प्रकृति में 'विद्युत चुंबकीय' होती हैं?
e: Gamma rays are high-energy photons. / गामा किरणें उच्च ऊर्जा वाले फोटॉन हैं।

q: What is the 'Charge' on a Beta-plus particle (Positron)?\nबीटा-प्लस कण (पॉजिट्रॉन) पर 'आवेश' क्या होता है?
e: It is the positive counterpart of an electron. / यह इलेक्ट्रॉन का धनात्मक प्रतिरूप है।

q: Which country is the largest producer of 'Thorium' in the world?\nविश्व में 'थोरियम' का सबसे बड़ा उत्पादक देश कौन सा है?
e: India has vast thorium reserves in monazite sands. / भारत में मोनाजाइट रेत में विशाल थोरियम भंडार हैं।

q: What is 'Nuclear Winter'?\n'परमाणु शीतकाल' (Nuclear Winter) क्या है?
o: Global cooling caused by nuclear war smoke | परमाणु युद्ध के कारण होने वाली वैश्विक ठंड
e: Smoke blocks sunlight, leading to a drastic drop in temperature. / धुआं सूरज की रोशनी को रोकता है, जिससे तापमान गिर जाता है।

q: What is the 'Mass-Energy Equivalence' equation proposed by Einstein?\nआइंस्टीन द्वारा प्रस्तावित 'द्रव्यमान-ऊर्जा तुल्यता' समीकरण क्या है?
e: It states that mass can be converted into energy and vice versa. / यह बताता है कि द्रव्यमान को ऊर्जा में बदला जा सकता है।

q: Which radioactive series starts with Thorium-232 and ends with Lead-208?\nकौन सी रेडियोधर्मी श्रृंखला थोरियम-232 से शुरू होती है और लेड-208 पर समाप्त होती है?
e: All mass numbers in this series are multiples of 4. / इस श्रृंखला की सभी द्रव्यमान संख्याएं 4 की गुणज होती हैं।

q: What is the 'Radioactive Displacement Law' (Soddy-Fajans Law) regarding Alpha decay?\nअल्फा क्षय के संबंध में 'रेडियोधर्मी विस्थापन नियम' क्या है?
o: Shifts 2 places left | 2 स्थान बाईं ओर
e: Atomic number decreases by 2, moving the element two positions back in the periodic table. / परमाणु संख्या 2 कम हो जाती है।

q: What is the 'Nuclear Force' characteristic?\n'नाभिकीय बल' की विशेषता क्या है?
o: Short range and Strongest | लघु परास और सबसे मजबूत
e: It acts between nucleons (p-p, n-n, p-n) over very short distances. / यह न्यूक्लियॉन के बीच बहुत कम दूरी पर कार्य करता है।

q: Which isotope is used to detect 'Leaks' in underground oil or water pipes?\nभूमिगत तेल या पानी के पाइपों में 'रिसाव' का पता लगाने के लिए किस समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Its radiation can be detected from outside the pipe to locate the leak. / इसके विकिरण का उपयोग रिसाव का स्थान खोजने के लिए किया जा सकता है।

q: What is the 'Packing Fraction' of a nucleus?\nनाभिक का 'पैकिंग अंश' (Packing Fraction) क्या है?
o: Mass defect per nucleon | प्रति न्यूक्लियॉन द्रव्यमान क्षति
e: It is a measure of the stability of the nucleus. / यह नाभिक की स्थिरता का एक माप है।

q: Which radiation is 'Deflected' most by a magnetic field?\nचुंबकीय क्षेत्र द्वारा कौन सा विकिरण सबसे अधिक 'विक्षेपित' होता है?
e: Beta particles are much lighter than Alpha particles, so they deflect more easily. / बीटा कण अल्फा कणों की तुलना में बहुत हल्के होते हैं।

q: What is 'Nuclear Isomer' of an element?\nकिसी तत्व का 'परमाणु समावयवी' क्या है?
o: Same A and Z, different life | समान A और Z, अलग आयु
e: They differ only in their nuclear energy states. / वे केवल अपनी परमाणु ऊर्जा अवस्थाओं में भिन्न होते हैं।

q: Which 'Coolant' is used in fast breeder reactors?\nफास्ट ब्रीडर रिएक्टरों में किस 'शीतलक' का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Liquid sodium has excellent heat transfer properties. / तरल सोडियम में उत्कृष्ट ऊष्मा स्थानांतरण गुण होते हैं।

q: What is 'Electron Capture' in nuclear chemistry?\nनाभिकीय रसायन में 'इलेक्ट्रॉन कैप्चर' क्या है?
o: Nucleus absorbs an inner shell electron | नाभिक आंतरिक कोश के इलेक्ट्रॉन को अवशोषित करता है
e: A proton combines with the electron to form a neutron. / एक प्रोटॉन इलेक्ट्रॉन के साथ मिलकर न्यूट्रॉन बनाता है।

q: What is the charge on a 'Gamma' ray photon?\nगामा किरण फोटॉन पर कितना आवेश होता है?
e: Gamma rays are neutral electromagnetic radiation. / गामा किरणें उदासीन विद्युत चुंबकीय विकिरण हैं।

q: Which isotope of Carbon is used in Carbon Dating?\nकार्बन डेटिंग में कार्बन के किस समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: C-14 is radioactive and decays over time, allowing age calculation. / C-14 रेडियोधर्मी है और समय के साथ क्षय होता है।

q: Which type of radiation is most dangerous when 'Ingested' (swallowed)?\nकौन सा विकिरण 'निगलने' पर सबसे खतरनाक होता है?
e: Alpha particles have the highest ionizing power and damage tissues from inside. / अल्फा कणों की आयनकारी शक्ति सबसे अधिक होती है।

q: Who is known as the 'Father of Nuclear Chemistry'?\n'नाभिकीय रसायन का जनक' किसे कहा जाता है?
e: Otto Hahn discovered nuclear fission in 1938. / ओटो हैन ने 1938 में परमाणु विखंडन की खोज की थी।

q: What is the function of 'Control Rods' in a nuclear reactor?\nपरमाणु रिएक्टर में 'नियंत्रण छड़ों' का कार्य क्या है?
o: To absorb excess neutrons | अतिरिक्त न्यूट्रॉनों को अवशोषित करना
e: Commonly made of Cadmium or Boron. / आमतौर पर कैडमियम या बोरॉन से बनी होती हैं।

q: What is the 'SI Unit' of radioactivity?\nरेडियोधर्मिता की 'SI इकाई' क्या है?
e: 1 Becquerel = 1 disintegration per second. / 1 बेकरेल = 1 विखंडन प्रति सेकंड।

q: What remains constant during 'Gamma' emission?\n'गामा' उत्सर्जन के दौरान क्या स्थिर रहता है?
o: Both A and B | A और B दोनों
e: Gamma emission only involves a change in the energy state of the nucleus. / गामा उत्सर्जन में केवल नाभिक की ऊर्जा अवस्था बदलती है।

q: Which radioactive isotope is used to determine the age of 'Rocks'?\n'चट्टानों' की आयु निर्धारित करने के लिए किस रेडियोधर्मी समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: U-238 has a very long half-life, suitable for geological dating. / यूरेनियम-238 की अर्ध-आयु बहुत लंबी होती है।

q: The 'Binding Energy' per nucleon is highest for which element?\nप्रति न्यूक्लियॉन 'बंधन ऊर्जा' किस तत्व के लिए उच्चतम है?
e: Iron-56 is the most stable nucleus. / आयरन-56 सबसे स्थिर नाभिक है।

q: What is 'Nuclear Fusion' source of energy in stars?\nतारों में 'परमाणु संलयन' ऊर्जा का स्रोत क्या है?
o: Hydrogen to Helium | हाइड्रोजन से हीलियम
e: Light nuclei combine to form heavier ones at extreme temperatures. / अत्यधिक तापमान पर हल्के नाभिक मिलकर भारी नाभिक बनाते हैं।

q: Which particle is emitted when a Neutron turns into a Proton in the nucleus?\nजब नाभिक में एक न्यूट्रॉन प्रोटॉन में बदलता है तो कौन सा कण उत्सर्जित होता है?
e: This process increases the atomic number by 1. / यह प्रक्रिया परमाणु संख्या में 1 की वृद्धि करती है।

q: What is 'Heavy Water' (D2O) used for in nuclear reactors?\nपरमाणु रिएक्टरों में 'भारी जल' का उपयोग किस लिए किया जाता है?
o: As a Moderator | मंदक के रूप में
e: It slows down neutrons so they can cause further fission. / यह न्यूट्रॉनों को धीमा करता है ताकि वे आगे विखंडन कर सकें।

q: Which radiation is used for 'Food Preservation' (Sterilization)?\n'खाद्य संरक्षण' के लिए किस विकिरण का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Gamma rays kill bacteria and parasites without heating the food. / गामा किरणें भोजन को गर्म किए बिना बैक्टीरिया को मार देती हैं।

q: What is the approximate 'Half-life' of Carbon-14?\nकार्बन-14 की अनुमानित 'अर्ध-आयु' क्या है?
e: This constant rate makes it perfect for dating archaeological finds. / यह पुरातत्व खोजों की आयु निर्धारण के लिए उपयुक्त है।

q: Which radioactive gas is found in homes and can cause Lung Cancer?\nघरों में कौन सी रेडियोधर्मी गैस पाई जाती है जो फेफड़ों के कैंसर का कारण बन सकती है?
e: Radon is a decay product of Uranium found in soil and rocks. / रेडॉन मिट्टी और चट्टानों में पाए जाने वाले यूरेनियम का क्षय उत्पाद है।

q: What happens to the 'Mass Number' during Alpha decay?\nअल्फा क्षय के दौरान 'द्रव्यमान संख्या' का क्या होता है?
o: Decreases by 4 | 4 कम हो जाती है
e: An alpha particle has a mass of 4 units ($2p + 2n$). / एक अल्फा कण का द्रव्यमान 4 इकाई होता है।

q: Which isotope is used as fuel in 'Nuclear Submarines'?\n'परमाणु पनडुब्बियों' में ईंधन के रूप में किस समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Enriched Uranium provides a high energy density for long voyages. / समृद्ध यूरेनियम लंबी यात्राओं के लिए उच्च ऊर्जा घनत्व प्रदान करता है।

q: What is a 'Positron'?\n'पॉजिट्रॉन' क्या है?
o: Anti-particle of Electron | इलेक्ट्रॉन का एंटी-पार्टिकल
e: It has the same mass as an electron but a positive charge. / इसका द्रव्यमान इलेक्ट्रॉन के समान होता है लेकिन आवेश धनात्मक होता है।

q: The 'Stability' of a nucleus depends on which ratio?\nनाभिक की 'स्थिरता' किस अनुपात पर निर्भर करती है?
e: For light elements, n/p = 1 is stable; for heavy, it increases. / हल्के तत्वों के लिए n/p = 1 स्थिर होता है।

q: Which radiation has the 'Lowest' penetrating power?\nकिस विकिरण की भेदन क्षमता 'न्यूनतम' होती है?
e: Alpha rays can be stopped by a thin sheet of paper. / अल्फा किरणों को कागज की एक पतली शीट द्वारा रोका जा सकता है।

q: What is 'Breeder Reactor'?\n'ब्रीडर रिएक्टर' क्या है?
o: Reactor that produces more fuel than it consumes | वह रिएक्टर जो खपत से अधिक ईंधन पैदा करता है
e: It converts non-fissile materials like U-238 into fissile Pu-239. / यह गैर-विखंडनीय सामग्री को विखंडनीय सामग्री में बदलता है।

q: Which 'Rare Earth' radioactive element was discovered by Marie Curie?\nमैरी क्यूरी ने किस 'दुर्लभ' रेडियोधर्मी तत्व की खोज की थी?
e: She also discovered Polonium and won two Nobel Prizes. / उन्होंने पोलोनियम की भी खोज की थी।

q: What is the 'Critical Mass' in a chain reaction?\nश्रृंखला अभिक्रिया में 'क्रांतिक द्रव्यमान' क्या है?
o: Min mass needed to sustain a chain reaction | श्रृंखला अभिक्रिया बनाए रखने के लिए न्यूनतम द्रव्यमान
e: Below this mass, the neutrons escape too fast to cause more fission. / इस द्रव्यमान से नीचे न्यूट्रॉन बहुत तेज़ी से बाहर निकल जाते हैं।

q: Which radioactive isotope is used to study the 'Thyroid Gland'?\n'थायरॉयड ग्रंथि' के अध्ययन के लिए किस रेडियोधर्मी समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Used for both diagnosis and treatment of thyroid cancer. / इसका उपयोग थायरॉयड कैंसर के निदान और उपचार दोनों के लिए किया जाता है।

q: What is the 'Magic Number' in nuclear physics?\nपरमाणु भौतिकी में 'मैजिक नंबर' क्या है?
o: Numbers of nucleons that lead to extra stability | न्यूक्लियॉन की संख्या जो अतिरिक्त स्थिरता देती है

q: Which radiation is 'Positively' charged and has the same mass as Helium?\nकौन सा विकिरण 'धनावेशित' है और इसका द्रव्यमान हीलियम के समान है?
e: Alpha particles are stripped Helium nuclei. / अल्फा कण हीलियम नाभिक होते हैं।

q: What is the 'Moderator' used in India's nuclear reactors (CANDU type)?\nभारत के परमाणु रिएक्टरों में किस 'मंदक' का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: India's pressurized heavy water reactors (PHWRs) use D2O. / भारत के PHWR रिएक्टर भारी जल का उपयोग करते हैं।

q: Which process is used in an 'Atomic Bomb'?\n'परमाणु बम' में किस प्रक्रिया का उपयोग किया जाता है?
o: Uncontrolled Nuclear Fission | अनियंत्रित परमाणु विखंडन
e: A rapid chain reaction releases massive energy. / एक तीव्र श्रृंखला अभिक्रिया भारी ऊर्जा मुक्त करती है।

q: What is 'Decay Constant' ($\\lambda$)?\\n'क्षय स्थिरांक' ($\\lambda$) क्या है?
o: Probability of decay per unit time | प्रति इकाई समय में क्षय की संभावना
e: Related to half-life by $T_{1/2} = 0.693 / \\lambda$. / यह अर्ध-आयु से संबंधित है।

q: Which radioactive isotope is used in 'Smoke Detectors'?\n'धुआं डिटेक्टरों' में किस रेडियोधर्मी समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: It ionizes air; smoke particles disrupt this current, triggering the alarm. / यह हवा को आयनित करता है।

q: What happens to the 'Atomic Number' during Beta-minus decay?\nबीटा-माइनस क्षय के दौरान 'परमाणु संख्या' का क्या होता है?
e: A neutron turns into a proton + electron. / एक न्यूट्रॉन प्रोटॉन + इलेक्ट्रॉन में बदल जाता है।

q: What is the 'Fuel' used in a Hydrogen Bomb?\nहाइड्रोजन बम में किस 'ईंधन' का उपयोग किया जाता है?
o: Isotopes of Hydrogen (Deuterium/Tritium) | हाइड्रोजन के समस्थानिक
e: Fusion of H-isotopes requires immense heat from a fission bomb. / इसके लिए विखंडन बम से अत्यधिक गर्मी की आवश्यकता होती है।

q: Which unit is used to measure 'Radiation Dose' absorbed by human tissue?\nमानव ऊतक द्वारा अवशोषित 'विकिरण खुराक' को मापने के लिए किस इकाई का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Sievert accounts for the biological effect of radiation. / सीवर्ट विकिरण के जैविक प्रभाव को दर्शाता है।

q: What is 'Nuclear Spallation'?\n'न्यूक्लियर स्पैलेशन' क्या है?
o: Ejection of many small fragments by high energy impact | उच्च ऊर्जा प्रहार से कई छोटे टुकड़ों का निकलना
e: Occurs when a nucleus is hit by a very high energy particle. / तब होता है जब नाभिक पर बहुत उच्च ऊर्जा वाले कण से प्रहार किया जाता है।

q: Which radioactive isotope is used as a 'Tracer' in agriculture?\nकृषि में 'ट्रेसर' के रूप में किस रेडियोधर्मी समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Used to study how plants absorb fertilizers. / यह अध्ययन करने के लिए कि पौधे उर्वरकों को कैसे अवशोषित करते हैं।

q: What is the charge of a 'Positron'?\n'पॉजिट्रॉन' का आवेश क्या होता है?
e: It is the antiparticle of an electron. / यह इलेक्ट्रॉन का प्रतिकण है।

q: Which radiation has the 'Highest' ionizing power?\nकिस विकिरण में 'उच्चतम' आयनकारी शक्ति होती है?
e: Because they are relatively large and double-charged. / क्योंकि वे तुलनात्मक रूप से बड़े और द्वि-आवेशित होते हैं।

q: What is 'Nuclear Isomerism'?\n'परमाणु समावयवता' क्या है?
o: Same A and Z but different energy states | समान A और Z लेकिन अलग ऊर्जा अवस्थाएँ
e: Metastable states are denoted by 'm' (e.g., Tc-99m). / मेटास्टेबल अवस्थाओं को 'm' द्वारा दर्शाया जाता है।

q: Which material is used to wrap 'Radioactive waste' for burial?\nदफनाने के लिए 'रेडियोधर्मी कचरे' को लपेटने के लिए किस सामग्री का उपयोग किया जाता है?
o: Lead and Concrete | सीसा और कंक्रीट
e: These block the escape of harmful radiations. / ये हानिकारक विकिरणों को बाहर निकलने से रोकते हैं।

q: What is 'Average Life' ($\tau$) of a radioactive substance?\nरेडियोधर्मी पदार्थ की 'औसत आयु' ($\tau$) क्या है?
e: It is the reciprocal of the decay constant. / यह क्षय स्थिरांक का व्युत्क्रम है।

q: Which 'Law' is followed by radioactive decay?\nरेडियोधर्मी क्षय किस 'नियम' का पालन करता है?
o: First order kinetics | प्रथम कोटि की बलगतिकी
e: The rate depends on the number of atoms present at that time. / दर उस समय मौजूद परमाणुओं की संख्या पर निर्भर करती है।

q: Which radioactive isotope is used as a 'Fuel' in the Curiosity Rover on Mars?\nमंगल ग्रह पर क्यूरियोसिटी रोवर में ईंधन के रूप में किस रेडियोधर्मी समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: It uses a Multi-Mission Radioisotope Thermoelectric Generator (MMRTG). / यह रेडियोआइसोटोप थर्मोइलेक्ट्रिक जनरेटर का उपयोग करता है।

q: What is 'Nuclear Spallation' used to produce?\n'परमाणु स्पैलेशन' (Nuclear Spallation) का उपयोग क्या उत्पन्न करने के लिए किया जाता है?
e: High-energy protons hit a heavy metal target to knock out neutrons. / उच्च ऊर्जा वाले प्रोटॉन न्यूट्रॉन को बाहर निकालने के लिए भारी धातु के लक्ष्य से टकराते हैं।

q: Which part of the 'Sun' is where nuclear fusion occurs?\nसूर्य के किस 'भाग' में परमाणु संलयन होता है?
e: The core has the extreme pressure and temperature (15 million °C) needed for fusion. / कोर में संलयन के लिए आवश्यक अत्यधिक दबाव और तापमान होता है।

q: What is the 'Decay Series' of Uranium-238 called?\nयूरेनियम-238 की 'क्षय श्रृंखला' को क्या कहा जाता है?
e: It ends at stable Lead-206. / यह स्थिर लेड-206 पर समाप्त होती है।

q: Which 'Mineral' is the main source of Uranium in India (Jaduguda mines)?\nभारत (जादुगुडा खदानों) में यूरेनियम का मुख्य स्रोत कौन सा 'खनिज' है?
e: Jaduguda in Jharkhand was the first uranium mine in India. / झारखंड का जादुगुडा भारत की पहली यूरेनियम खदान थी।

q: What is 'K-Electron Capture' effect on the Atomic Number?\nपरमाणु संख्या पर 'K-इलेक्ट्रॉन कैप्चर' का क्या प्रभाव पड़ता है?
o: Decreases by 1 | 1 कम हो जाती है
e: A proton turns into a neutron by capturing an electron. / इलेक्ट्रॉन को कैप्चर करके प्रोटॉन न्यूट्रॉन में बदल जाता है।

q: Which radiation is 'Highest' in ionizing power but 'Lowest' in penetration?\nकिस विकिरण की आयनकारी शक्ति 'उच्चतम' लेकिन भेदन क्षमता 'न्यूनतम' होती है?
e: Alpha particles are heavy and double-charged, so they interact strongly with matter. / अल्फा कण भारी और द्वि-आवेशित होते हैं।

q: What is 'Criticality' in a nuclear reactor?\nपरमाणु रिएक्टर में 'क्रिटिकैलिटी' (Criticality) क्या है?
o: Balanced chain reaction | संतुलित श्रृंखला अभिक्रिया
e: It means the number of neutrons produced equals the number lost. / इसका अर्थ है कि पैदा हुए न्यूट्रॉन और खोए हुए न्यूट्रॉन की संख्या बराबर है।

q: Which radioactive isotope is used in 'Brachytherapy' for eye cancer?\nआंखों के कैंसर के लिए 'ब्रेकीथेरेपी' में किस रेडियोधर्मी समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Plaques containing I-125 are stitched to the eye to treat the tumor. / ट्यूमर के इलाज के लिए I-125 वाले प्लेक आंख पर लगाए जाते हैं।

q: What is the 'Average Life' ($\\tau$) of a radioactive element with half-life $T_{1/2}$?\nअर्ध-आयु $T_{1/2}$ वाले रेडियोधर्मी तत्व की 'औसत आयु' ($\\tau$) क्या है?
e: Average life is always longer than half-life. / औसत आयु हमेशा अर्ध-आयु से लंबी होती है।

q: Which radioactive isotope is used to determine the age of 'Organic remains' like wood or bone?\nलकड़ी या हड्डी जैसे 'जैविक अवशेषों' की आयु निर्धारित करने के लिए किस रेडियोधर्मी समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: C-14 has a half-life of 5730 years and is absorbed by living organisms. / जीवित जीव C-14 को अवशोषित करते हैं।

q: What is the function of the 'Coolant' in a nuclear reactor?\nपरमाणु रिएक्टर में 'शीतलक' (Coolant) का क्या कार्य है?
o: To absorb heat from the core | कोर से ऊष्मा को अवशोषित करना
e: It transfers thermal energy to produce steam for turbines. / यह टर्बाइन के लिए भाप बनाने हेतु तापीय ऊर्जा स्थानांतरित करता है।

q: What is the 'SI Unit' of absorbed radiation dose?\nअवशोषित विकिरण खुराक की 'SI इकाई' क्या है?
e: 1 Gray is the absorption of one joule of radiation energy per kilogram of matter. / 1 ग्रे प्रति किलोग्राम पदार्थ में एक जूल विकिरण ऊर्जा का अवशोषण है।

q: Which type of 'Nuclear Reaction' is responsible for the energy of a Hydrogen Bomb?\nहाइड्रोजन बम की ऊर्जा के लिए किस प्रकार की 'परमाणु अभिक्रिया' जिम्मेदार है?
e: It involves the fusion of isotopes of hydrogen at very high temperatures. / इसमें बहुत उच्च तापमान पर हाइड्रोजन के समस्थानिकों का संलयन शामिल है।

q: Which 'Rare Gas' is produced during the radioactive decay of Radium?\nरेडियम के रेडियोधर्मी क्षय के दौरान कौन सी 'दुर्लभ गैस' उत्पन्न होती है?
e: Radon-222 is a decay product of Radium-226. / रेडॉन-222 रेडियम-226 का क्षय उत्पाद है।

q: What is 'Nuclear Waste' primarily stored in for safety?\nसुरक्षा के लिए 'परमाणु कचरे' को मुख्य रूप से किसमें संग्रहित किया जाता है?
e: Lead and concrete are effective at shielding harmful radiation. / सीसा और कंक्रीट हानिकारक विकिरण को रोकने में प्रभावी हैं।

q: Which isotope is used in 'Brachytherapy' to treat prostate cancer?\nप्रोस्टेट कैंसर के इलाज के लिए 'ब्रेकीथेरेपी' में किस समस्थानिक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Radioactive seeds of I-125 are implanted directly into the tissue. / I-125 के रेडियोधर्मी बीजों को सीधे ऊतक में लगाया जाता है।

q: What is the 'Half-life' of Phosphorus-32 used in agriculture?\nकृषि में प्रयुक्त फास्फोरस-32 की 'अर्ध-आयु' क्या है?
e: It is used as a tracer to study phosphorus uptake by plants. / इसका उपयोग पौधों द्वारा फास्फोरस ग्रहण करने के अध्ययन के लिए किया जाता है।

q: Which 'Discovery' led to the development of the Atomic Bomb?\nकिस 'खोज' के कारण परमाणु बम का विकास हुआ?
e: The discovery of fission by Otto Hahn and Fritz Strassmann. / ओटो हैन द्वारा विखंडन की खोज।

q: What is 'Nuclear Enrichment'?\n'परमाणु संवर्धन' (Nuclear Enrichment) क्या है?
o: Increasing the percent of U-235 in uranium | U-235 के प्रतिशत को बढ़ाना
e: Natural uranium has only 0.7% U-235; enrichment increases it for use in reactors. / प्राकृतिक यूरेनियम में केवल 0.7% U-235 होता है।

q: Which gas is evolved during the manufacture of 'Polyurethane' foam used in mattresses?
e: CO2 acts as a blowing agent to create the 'bubbles' or foam structure.

q: What is the 'Monomer' of Teflon?
e: Tetrafluoroethene ($CF_2=CF_2$) polymerizes to form Teflon.

q: Which synthetic fiber was the first to be made entirely from 'Chemicals' (Coal, Air, and Water)?
e: Nylon is a fully synthetic polyamide fiber developed in 1935.

q: What is 'Orlon' (Acrylic fiber) used as a substitute for?
e: Acrylic fibers are warm, lightweight, and soft, mimicking the properties of wool.

q: Which chemical is used as a 'Plasticizer' to make PVC flexible for wires?
e: Phthalates fit between polymer chains, allowing them to slide past each other.

q: What is 'Neoprene' primarily used for?
e: Neoprene is a synthetic rubber that is more resistant to oil and heat than natural rubber.

q: Which part of a 'Soap' molecule is attracted to water?
e: The ionic 'head' is water-loving, while the long hydrocarbon 'tail' is oil-loving.

q: What are 'Micelles' in the context of cleaning?
o: Clusters of soap molecules surrounding dirt
e: Micelles trap grease in the center, allowing it to be washed away with water.

q: Which 'Polyamide' is used for making toothbrush bristles?
e: Nylon's strength and elasticity make it perfect for bristles.

q: What is 'Melamine' used for in the kitchen?
e: Melamine-formaldehyde is a hard, fire-resistant plastic used for dinnerware.

q: Which substance is added to 'Detergents' to make them 'Whiter' (Optical brighteners)?
e: These dyes absorb UV light and re-emit blue light, making clothes look brighter.

q: What is 'Polyethylene' (Polythene) made from?
e: Ethene molecules join in long chains to form Polythene.

q: Which 'Natural Fiber' is a protein fiber obtained from silkworms?
e: Silk is composed primarily of the protein Fibroin.

q: What is the 'Main Constituent' of Jute, a major crop of North Bihar?
e: Jute is a lignocellulosic fiber, making it strong but biodegradable.

q: Which chemical is used to 'Degum' silk?
e: The gum (Sericin) is removed by boiling in a soap solution to make the silk soft.

q: What is 'Buna-S' synthetic rubber?
e: Buna-S is widely used for making automobile tires.

q: Which 'Polysaccharide' is used to make Rayon?
e: Rayon is regenerated cellulose.

q: What is 'PMMA' (Lucite or Plexiglass) used for?
o: Substitute for glass in aircraft windows
e: It is a transparent thermoplastic that is lighter and more shatter-resistant than glass.

q: Which fiber is known as the 'Golden Fiber' of India?
e: Jute is called the golden fiber due to its color and high economic value.

q: What is 'Biodegradable' plastic?
o: Plastic that can be broken down by bacteria
e: Examples include PLA (Polylactic acid) and PHB.

q: Which chemical is used to 'Mercerize' cotton to increase its strength and luster?
e: Treatment with NaOH makes cotton fibers swell and become shinier.

q: What is 'Styrofoam' chemically?
e: It is used for insulation and packaging material.

q: Which fiber is most 'Hygroscopic' (absorbs most moisture)?
e: Wool can absorb up to 30% of its weight in moisture without feeling wet.

q: What is 'Condensation Polymerization'?
o: Joining monomers while losing a small molecule (like Water)
e: Nylon and Polyester are formed by condensation polymerization.

q: Which substance is used to 'Bleach' hair industrially?
e: H2O2 is a common oxidizing bleach used in cosmetics.

q: What is 'Soap' chemically?
o: Sodium or Potassium salt of long-chain fatty acids
e: Sodium Stearate is a classic example of soap.

q: Which gas was responsible for the 'Bhopal Gas Tragedy' involving the production of pesticides?
e: MIC is an intermediate in the production of carbamate pesticides.

q: What is 'Tery-cot' fabric a mixture of?
e: It combines the strength of synthetic fiber with the comfort of cotton.

q: Which of the following is known as the 'Control Center' of the cell?
e: The Nucleus contains genetic material (DNA) and coordinates cell activities.

q: Which organ in the human body has the highest power of regeneration?
e: The Liver is the only internal human organ that can regenerate itself completely.

q: Protein digestion starts in which part of the digestive tract?
e: Pepsin enzyme in the stomach starts the breakdown of proteins.

q: The 'Fluid Mosaic Model' explains the structure of which part?
e: Singer and Nicolson proposed this model for the Cell Membrane in 1972.

q: Which acid is present in the human stomach to help digestion?
e: HCl kills bacteria and creates an acidic environment for enzymes.

q: Which cell organelle is responsible for lipid synthesis?
e: Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum (SER) synthesizes lipids and steroids.

q: Emulsification of fats is done by which substance?
e: Bile juice breaks down large fat globules into smaller droplets.

q: The longest part of the human digestive system is:
e: The small intestine is about 6-7 meters long, though narrow in diameter.

q: Which organelle is called the 'Protein Factory' of the cell?
e: Ribosomes are the site of protein synthesis in all living cells.

q: The yellow color of human feces is due to which pigment?
e: Bilirubin is a byproduct of red blood cell breakdown excreted in bile.

q: Which part of the digestive system absorbs water and minerals from undigested food?
e: The primary function of the large intestine is to absorb water, salts, and some vitamins from the remaining indigestible food matter.

q: In Jaundice, which organ is primarily affected?
e: Jaundice is caused by the buildup of bilirubin in the blood, indicating that the liver is not filtering blood properly.

q: Which of the following prevents the entry of food into the windpipe (trachea)?
e: The epiglottis is a flap of cartilage that flips down during swallowing to cover the opening of the windpipe.

q: Vitamins are absorbed primarily in which part of the small intestine?
e: While digestion starts in the duodenum, the final absorption of Vitamin B12 and bile salts happens in the Ileum.

q: Which disorder is characterized by the inflammation of the liver?
e: 'Hepa' refers to the liver. Hepatitis is the inflammation of liver tissue, often caused by viruses.

q: The semi-solid mass of food formed in the stomach is called:
e: Food mixed with gastric juices in the stomach is called Chyme. (Bolus is in the mouth; Chyle is in the small intestine).

q: Which enzyme converts proteins into Peptones in the stomach?
e: Pepsin is secreted in an inactive form (pepsinogen) and becomes active in the presence of HCl to break down proteins.

q: The 'Lacteals' in the small intestine are responsible for the absorption of:
e: Lacteals are lymphatic capillaries that absorb dietary fats in the villi of the small intestine.

q: Which part of the Large Intestine hosts symbiotic bacteria?
e: The cecum is a small blind sac which hosts certain symbiotic micro-organisms that help in digestion.

q: Protein deficiency in children leads to which disease?
e: Kwashiorkor is severe protein malnutrition characterized by edema (swelling) and an enlarged liver.

q: Which part of the digestive system absorbs water and minerals from undigested food?
e: The primary function of the large intestine is to absorb water and electrolytes from the indigestible food matter before it is excreted.

q: In Jaundice, which organ is primarily affected due to the buildup of bilirubin?
e: Jaundice indicates a malfunction of the liver, where it fails to filter bilirubin from the blood, causing yellowing of skin and eyes.

q: Which of the following prevents the entry of food into the windpipe (trachea) during swallowing?
e: The epiglottis is a cartilaginous flap that acts as a switch, covering the windpipe while swallowing to prevent choking.

q: The 'Lacteals' found in the villi of the small intestine are responsible for the absorption of:
e: Lacteals are specialized lymphatic capillaries that absorb dietary fats (fatty acids and glycerol) that are too large for blood capillaries.

q: What is the semi-solid mass of food called once it leaves the stomach and enters the small intestine?
e: Chyme is the thick, acidic fluid consisting of gastric juices and partly digested food. Bolus is what we swallow from the mouth.

q: Which enzyme converts proteins into peptones and proteoses in the stomach?
e: Pepsin is the primary protein-digesting enzyme of the stomach, active only in a high-acid (low pH) environment.

q: Which part of the Large Intestine hosts symbiotic bacteria that produce Vitamin K?
e: The cecum is a blind pouch that houses beneficial bacteria which aid in further digestion and vitamin synthesis.

q: Severe protein deficiency in children, characterized by a 'pot belly' and thin limbs, is called:
e: Kwashiorkor is caused by sufficient calorie intake but severe protein deficiency. Marasmus is a deficiency of both calories and protein.

q: Which cells of the gastric glands secrete 'Castle's Intrinsic Factor' for Vitamin B12 absorption?
e: Oxyntic cells secrete both HCl and Intrinsic Factor. Without this factor, Vitamin B12 cannot be absorbed, leading to anemia.

q: The 'Islets of Langerhans' which produce insulin are located in which organ?
e: The pancreas is a dual-purpose gland; the Islets of Langerhans perform the endocrine function by secreting hormones like insulin and glucagon.

q: Which digestive juice lacks enzymes but is essential for the digestion of fats?
e: Bile juice contains no enzymes. It contains bile salts that emulsify fats, breaking them into tiny droplets for lipase to act upon.

q: Glands of Brunner (Duodenal glands) are found in which layer of the duodenum?
e: Brunner's glands are located in the sub-mucosa of the duodenum and secrete alkaline mucus to neutralize stomach acid.

q: Which hormone triggers the contraction of the Gallbladder to release bile?
e: CCK is released by the small intestine when fats enter, signaling the gallbladder to squeeze bile into the duodenum.

q: The 'Crypts of Lieberkuhn' are found in:
e: These are tubular glands found between the bases of the villi in the small intestine that secrete intestinal juice.

q: Which of the following is an autoimmune disorder where the body attacks the lining of the small intestine due to gluten?
e: Celiac disease is a serious immune reaction to eating gluten, a protein found in wheat, barley, and rye.

q: What is the main function of the Colon?
o: Preparation of feces and water absorption
e: The colon concentrates waste material by removing water and forming solid feces for elimination.

q: Which enzyme converts Maltose into two molecules of Glucose?
e: Maltase is an intestinal enzyme that specifically breaks down the disaccharide maltose into simple glucose units.

q: The wave-like muscular contractions that push food through the digestive tract are called:
e: Peristalsis is the involuntary contraction and relaxation of longitudinal and circular muscles throughout the gut.

q: Which organ stores glycogen and helps in maintaining blood glucose levels?
e: The liver converts excess glucose into glycogen for storage (glycogenesis) and releases it back when blood sugar is low.

q: What is the dental formula for milk teeth (deciduous teeth) in human children?
e: Children have 20 milk teeth. They lack premolars and the third molar (wisdom tooth). The formula is 2-1-0-2.

q: Which of the following is the largest gland in the human body?
e: The liver is the largest gland, weighing about 1.5 kg in an adult. It performs over 500 vital functions.

q: The 'Sphincter of Oddi' guards the opening of which duct?
e: The Sphincter of Oddi controls the flow of digestive juices (bile and pancreatic juice) into the duodenum.

q: Which enzyme is NOT found in pancreatic juice?
e: Enterokinase is an intestinal enzyme that activates trypsinogen into trypsin. The others are pancreatic enzymes.

q: Which part of the small intestine is the longest and highly coiled?
e: The Ileum is the final and longest segment of the small intestine, measuring about 3.5 meters.

q: The condition 'Marasmus' is caused by the deficiency of:
e: Marasmus is severe malnutrition characterized by energy deficiency, affecting infants under one year.

q: Which pigment gives the characteristic brown color to human feces?
e: Stercobilin is a biochemical catabolite of hemoglobin that provides the brown color to stool.

q: The movement of water across the intestinal wall occurs by:
e: Water absorption in the intestines follows the osmotic gradient created by the absorption of nutrients and ions.

q: Which part of the tooth is the hardest and lacks blood vessels?
e: Enamel is the most highly mineralized substance in the body and is non-living once formed.

q: The secretion of 'Secretin' hormone is triggered by:
e: Acidic chyme entering the duodenum stimulates S-cells to release Secretin, which signals the pancreas to release bicarbonate.

q: Which of the following is an example of mechanical digestion?
e: Mastication is the physical breakdown of food into smaller pieces by the teeth.

q: Where are 'Peyer's Patches' (lymphoid tissue) located?
e: Peyer's patches are organized lymphoid follicles found in the submucosa of the small intestine, mainly the ileum.

q: Which enzyme converts Chymotrypsinogen into active Chymotrypsin?
e: Once trypsin is activated by enterokinase, it goes on to activate chymotrypsinogen and procarboxypeptidase.

q: The structural and functional unit of the liver is the:
e: Hepatic lobules are small, hexagonal functional units of the liver consisting of plates of hepatocytes.

q: What is the function of the 'Serosa' layer in the alimentary canal?
e: The serosa is the outermost layer that secretes serous fluid to reduce friction during digestive movements.

q: Absorption of alcohol and simple sugars starts in which organ?
e: While most nutrients are absorbed in the small intestine, alcohol and some drugs are absorbed directly through the stomach lining.

q: Which of the following describes the human dentition?
e: Humans have teeth in sockets (Thecodont), two sets of teeth (Diphyodont), and different types of teeth (Heterodont).

q: Which organelle is highly developed in liver cells for detoxification?
e: Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum (SER) plays a major role in detoxifying drugs and poisons in the liver.

q: What is the primary site of fat digestion in the human body?
e: The small intestine receives bile for emulsification and pancreatic lipase for the final breakdown of fats.

q: What is the scientific name of Vitamin A?
e: Retinol is essential for the formation of rhodopsin in the eyes, which helps in low-light vision.

q: Which vitamin is also known as 'Sunshine Vitamin'?
e: Vitamin D is synthesized in the skin when exposed to ultraviolet (UV) rays from sunlight.

q: Deficiency of which vitamin causes 'Pellagra'?
e: Pellagra is caused by a deficiency of Niacin (B3), characterized by the '3 Ds': Dermatitis, Diarrhea, and Dementia.

q: Which mineral is essential for the synthesis of Hemoglobin in the blood?
e: Iron is a core component of the heme group in hemoglobin, which carries oxygen in the blood.

q: Which enzyme converts Milk into Curd in the human stomach (infants)?
e: Rennin (Chymosin) is secreted by gastric glands in infants to coagulate milk for better digestion.

q: Which vitamin contains a metal ion (Cobalt)?
e: Vitamin B12 is also known as Cyanocobalamin because it contains the metal Cobalt at its center.

q: Which of the following vitamins is water-soluble?
e: Vitamins B and C are water-soluble, while A, D, E, and K are fat-soluble (KEDA).

q: The hormone 'Secretin' is produced by which organ?
e: Secretin is produced by the S-cells of the duodenum to regulate the pH of the small intestine.

q: Which mineral prevents 'Dental Caries' (tooth decay)?
e: Fluorine (as Fluoride) strengthens tooth enamel and makes it more resistant to acid attacks from bacteria.

q: Which part of the digestive system has no digestive function?
e: The esophagus is a simple muscular tube that transports food via peristalsis without secreting enzymes.

q: Scientific name of Vitamin C is:
e: Ascorbic acid is vital for collagen synthesis and immune system function.

q: Ariboflavinosis is caused by the deficiency of which vitamin?
e: Riboflavin (B2) deficiency causes sores on the mouth corners and inflammation of the tongue.

q: Which organelle is called the 'Garbage Disposal' of the cell?
e: Lysosomes digest waste materials and cellular debris using hydrolytic enzymes.

q: The maximum amount of energy is released by the oxidation of:
e: Fats provide roughly 9 kcal//gram, which is more than double the energy provided by carbs or proteins (4 kcal//gram).

q: Which vitamin helps in the absorption of Calcium in the intestine?
e: Vitamin D promotes the synthesis of calcium-binding proteins in the intestinal wall.

q: Which of the following is known as the 'Brain of the Cell'?
e: The nucleus coordinates all cell activities like growth and reproduction and contains the cell's genetic information.

q: Which cell organelle is responsible for the synthesis of ATP?
e: Mitochondria are the site of cellular respiration where glucose is converted into ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate).

q: What is the main component of the plant cell wall?
e: Cellulose is a complex carbohydrate that provides structural support and rigidity to plant cells.

q: Which organelle is found in plant cells but absent in animal cells?
e: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and are responsible for photosynthesis, which animals do not perform.

q: The 'Powerhouse of the Cell' is:
e: Mitochondria produce the energy currency of the cell through biochemical reactions.

q: Which scientist discovered the cell for the first time in 1665?
e: Robert Hooke observed cork cells under a primitive microscope and coined the term 'cell'.

q: Which cell organelle is called the 'Protein Factory'?
e: Ribosomes are the sites where amino acids are assembled into proteins based on mRNA instructions.

q: The jelly-like substance present inside the cell membrane is:
e: Cytoplasm holds the organelles in place and is where most cellular metabolic activities occur.

q: Which organelle is involved in the formation of Lysosomes?
e: The Golgi apparatus processes and packages proteins and lipids into vesicles, including lysosomes.

q: Which of the following is a prokaryotic cell?
e: Bacteria lack a defined nucleus and membrane-bound organelles, making them prokaryotic.

q: Who proposed the 'Cell Theory'?
e: Schleiden and Schwann stated that all plants and animals are composed of cells and that the cell is the basic unit of life.

q: The site of lipid synthesis in the cell is:
e: Smooth ER (SER) is responsible for the synthesis of lipids, phospholipids, and steroids.

q: What is the primary function of the 'Vacuole' in plant cells?
e: In plants, vacuoles are very large and store water, nutrients, and waste while maintaining cell pressure (turgor).

q: Which part of the cell is known as the 'Gateway of the Cell'?
e: The plasma membrane is selectively permeable, controlling what enters and leaves the cell.

q: Who discovered the Nucleus in the cell in 1831?
e: Robert Brown was the first to describe the nucleus as a distinct part of the cell.

q: The 'Suicidal Bags' of the cell are:
e: Lysosomes contain powerful digestive enzymes that can digest the cell itself if it malfunctions.

q: Which cell organelle is known as the 'Packaging and Dispatching Unit'?
e: The Golgi apparatus modifies, sorts, and packages proteins for secretion or delivery to other organelles.

q: DNA is primarily found in which cell organelle?
e: Most DNA is located in the nucleus (nuclear DNA), while a small amount is in mitochondria.

q: Omnis cellula-e cellula (All cells arise from pre-existing cells) was stated by:
e: Rudolf Virchow expanded the cell theory by explaining how new cells are formed.

q: Which stage of Mitosis is known as the 'Preparatory Phase'?
e: Interphase is the longest phase where the cell grows and replicates its DNA before division starts.

q: The 'G-cells' of the stomach secrete which hormone?
e: Gastrin stimulates the parietal cells to secrete Hydrochloric acid (HCl) for digestion.

q: In which stage of Meiosis does 'Crossing Over' occur?
e: Crossing over happens in the Pachytene stage of Prophase I, leading to genetic variation.

q: Which enzyme is responsible for the activation of Trypsinogen?
e: Enterokinase, secreted by the intestinal mucosa, converts inactive trypsinogen into active trypsin.

q: The 'Middle Lamella' of plant cells is mainly composed of:
e: Calcium pectate acts as a glue that holds neighboring plant cells together.

q: Which part of the small intestine is the primary site for iron absorption?
e: Most dietary iron is absorbed in the duodenum, the first part of the small intestine.

q: Which cell organelle is responsible for 'Photorespiration' in plants?
e: Peroxisomes, along with chloroplasts and mitochondria, are involved in the C2 cycle (Photorespiration).

q: The 'Dental Formula' for a 17-year-old human (before wisdom teeth) is:
e: Before the third molars (wisdom teeth) erupt, the formula is 2-1-2-2.

q: Which protein is found in the 'Microtubules' of the cytoskeleton?
e: Microtubules are composed of alpha and beta tubulin dimers and help in cell shape and division.

q: A deficiency of Vitamin B12 (Cyanocobalamin) leads to which type of Anemia?
e: Vitamin B12 is crucial for RBC maturation; its lack causes Pernicious Anemia.

q: Which stage of Mitosis involves the alignment of chromosomes at the equator?
e: During Metaphase, chromosomes attach to spindle fibers and line up at the metaphase plate.

q: The breakdown of glucose into pyruvate occurs in which part of the cell?
e: Glycolysis (the first step of respiration) takes place in the cytoplasm.

q: Which part of the tooth contains the 'Odontoblasts'?
e: Odontoblasts are cells in the dental pulp that produce dentine.

q: Which organelle is called the 'Dictyosome' in plant cells?
e: In plants, the Golgi bodies are simpler and scattered, often referred to as Dictyosomes.

q: The primary bile acids are synthesized from:
e: The liver converts cholesterol into primary bile acids like cholic acid.

q: Which cell has the longest lifespan in the human body?
e: Most neurons are formed before birth and can last for the entire lifetime of an individual.

q: The 'Rugae' (folds) are found in the inner lining of which organ?
e: Rugae allow the stomach to expand significantly after a large meal.

q: Which ion is essential for the binding of mRNA to ribosomes?
e: Magnesium ions (Mg2+) are required to maintain the structural integrity of ribosomes.

q: Which organelle is known as the 'Pleomorphic' organelle because it can change its shape?
e: Mitochondria are dynamic and can undergo fission and fusion, changing their shape depending on the cell's energy needs.

q: The 'Islets of Langerhans' contain Alpha cells that secrete which hormone?
e: Alpha cells secrete Glucagon (raises blood sugar), while Beta cells secrete Insulin (lowers blood sugar).

q: Which scientist coined the term 'Protoplasm' in 1839?
e: J.E. Purkinje coined the term Protoplasm for the fluid substance of the cell.

q: The digestion of DNA and RNA is carried out by which pancreatic enzymes?
e: Nucleases (DNase and RNase) in the pancreatic juice break down nucleic acids into nucleotides.

q: Which organelle is responsible for the 'Acrosome' formation in a human sperm cell?
e: The Acrosome, which helps the sperm penetrate the egg, is a modified Golgi apparatus.

q: The 'Brunner's Glands' are found in which specific part of the small intestine?
e: Brunner's glands are unique to the duodenum and secrete alkaline mucus to protect against stomach acid.

q: Which of the following contains its own DNA and Ribosomes (Semi-autonomous)?
e: Chloroplasts and Mitochondria have their own DNA and 70S ribosomes, allowing them to make some of their own proteins.

q: What is the common name for the 'Upper Esophageal Sphincter'?
e: The cricopharyngeal sphincter prevents air from entering the esophagus during breathing.

q: The 'Tonoplast' is the membrane surrounding which organelle?
e: In plant cells, the large central vacuole is bound by a single membrane called the Tonoplast.

q: Which mineral is required for the activation of the 'Salivary Amylase' enzyme?
e: Chloride ions (Cl-) act as activators for salivary amylase (ptyalin) in the mouth.

q: Which stage of the Cell Cycle involves DNA Replication?
e: In the S (Synthesis) phase, the amount of DNA per cell doubles, though the chromosome number remains the same.

q: The 'Hepatopancreatic ampulla' is also known as the:
e: The Ampulla of Vater is where the bile duct and the pancreatic duct join to enter the duodenum.

q: Which cell organelle is responsible for the detoxification of drugs in the Liver?
e: Smooth ER contains enzymes that catalyze reactions to make drugs and toxins water-soluble for excretion.

q: What is the pH of Saliva in a healthy human?
e: Saliva is slightly acidic to neutral (average pH 6.8), which is ideal for the action of amylase.

q: Which organelle is absent in Prokaryotic cells?
e: Prokaryotes lack membrane-bound organelles like Mitochondria, Golgi bodies, and Lysosomes.

q: The process of 'Defecation' is controlled by which part of the Nervous System?
e: The involuntary part is parasympathetic, while the external anal sphincter is under voluntary (somatic) control.

q: Which type of Ribosomes are found in the Cytoplasm of Eukaryotic cells?
e: Eukaryotic cytoplasmic ribosomes are 80S (composed of 60S and 40S subunits).

q: What is the average length of the 'Esophagus' in an adult human?
e: The esophagus is a muscular tube approximately 25 cm (10 inches) long.

q: In which stage of the cell cycle does the synthesis of proteins and RNA occur for the first time?
e: The G1 (Gap 1) phase is the interval between mitosis and the initiation of DNA replication, where the cell is metabolically active and grows.

q: The reflex action of 'Vomiting' is controlled by the center located in which part of the brain?
e: The vomit center is located in the medulla oblongata, which regulates involuntary functions like breathing and heart rate.

q: Which stage of Prophase I in Meiosis involves the pairing of homologous chromosomes (Synapsis)?
e: During Zygotene, homologous chromosomes start pairing together, a process called synapsis, forming a bivalent.

q: 'Constipation' is a digestive disorder primarily related to the malfunction of which organ?
e: In constipation, the feces are retained within the colon (large intestine) as the bowel movements occur irregularly.

q: The 'Spindle Fibers' in animal cells are formed by which organelle?
e: Centrioles move to opposite poles during cell division and help organize the spindle apparatus.

q: Which enzyme is deficient in a person suffering from 'Lactose Intolerance'?
e: Lactase is needed to break down lactose (milk sugar). Its deficiency causes bloating and diarrhea after consuming dairy.

q: In which stage of Mitosis do the sister chromatids separate and move to opposite poles?
e: During Anaphase, the centromeres split, and sister chromatids are pulled toward opposite ends of the cell.

q: Which of the following is an example of an 'Exocrine' secretion of the Pancreas?
e: Pancreatic juice containing enzymes is an exocrine secretion released into ducts. Hormones like insulin are endocrine.

q: The 'X-shaped' structures formed during the Diplotene stage of Meiosis are called:
e: Chiasmata are the visible sites of crossing over between non-sister chromatids of homologous chromosomes.

q: The sensation of 'Heartburn' (Acid Reflux) is caused by the failure of which sphincter?
e: When the cardiac sphincter doesn't close properly, stomach acid flows back into the esophagus, causing a burning sensation.

q: What is the result of 'Meiosis' at the end of the process?
e: Meiosis is a reduction division that produces four haploid daughter cells, each with genetic variations.

q: Which condition is characterized by the inability of the body to absorb nutrients, leading to weight loss?
e: Malabsorption syndrome occurs when the small intestine cannot adequately absorb nutrients like proteins, fats, and vitamins.

q: Which stage of Mitosis is essentially the reverse of Prophase?
e: In Telophase, the nuclear envelope reforms and chromosomes de-condense, which is the opposite of what happens in Prophase.

q: The hormone 'Ghrelin', often called the 'Hunger Hormone', is primarily produced by:
e: Ghrelin is secreted by the stomach lining when it is empty, signaling the brain to increase appetite.

q: What is 'Cytokinesis'?
e: Cytokinesis is the physical process of cell division, which divides the cytoplasm of a parental cell into two daughter cells.

q: Which digestive enzyme is active at the lowest (most acidic) pH?
e: Pepsin works optimally at a pH of around 1.5 to 2.0 in the stomach.

q: During which phase of Meiosis do homologous chromosomes separate, while sister chromatids remain together?
e: In Anaphase I, the bivalents separate, but the sister chromatids stay attached at their centromeres.

q: The 'Haustra' are small pouches caused by sacculation, which give the ________ its segmented appearance.
e: Haustra are characteristic of the colon and are formed by the contraction of the taenia coli muscles.

q: Which of the following is known as the 'Resting Phase' of the cell cycle, although the cell is metabolically active?
e: Interphase is often called the resting phase because the cell isn't dividing, but it is actually preparing by replicating DNA and synthesizing proteins.

q: The 'Peyer's Patches' found in the ileum are a part of which system?
e: Peyer's patches are lymphoid follicles that monitor bacterial populations and prevent the growth of pathogenic bacteria in the intestines.

q: In which stage of the cell cycle do cells exit the cycle and enter a quiescent state?
e: Cells in the G0 phase are metabolically active but no longer proliferate unless called upon (e.g., heart or nerve cells).

q: The movement of food in the esophagus is regulated by which type of muscle?
e: The upper third of the esophagus is skeletal (voluntary), while the lower two-thirds are smooth muscle (involuntary).

q: Which protein complex holds the sister chromatids together at the centromere?
e: Cohesin proteins form a ring-like structure that keeps sister chromatids together until they are ready to separate in Anaphase.

q: Which of the following is NOT a part of the 'Large Intestine'?
e: The Duodenum is the first part of the small intestine. The large intestine consists of the caecum, colon, and rectum.

q: The 'Colchicine' drug inhibits cell division by interfering with:
e: Colchicine prevents the polymerization of tubulin into microtubules, effectively stopping mitosis at metaphase.

q: Which cells in the human body do NOT undergo cell division after maturity?
e: Most neurons (nerve cells) enter the G0 phase permanently and do not divide once the nervous system is developed.

q: What is the function of the 'Gallbladder'?
e: The liver produces bile, but the gallbladder stores it and removes water to make it more concentrated for fat digestion.

q: Which stage of Meiosis is responsible for the 'Reduction' of chromosome number?
e: Meiosis I separates homologous chromosomes, reducing the number from diploid (2n) to haploid (n).

q: Which of the following describes the pH of the Small Intestine?
e: Bicarbonate from the pancreas neutralizes stomach acid, making the small intestine slightly alkaline (pH 7.5 - 8.5).

q: What is the 'Kinetochore'?
o: A protein structure on the centromere for spindle attachment
e: The kinetochore is a complex of proteins associated with the centromere of a chromosome during cell division.

q: The condition where the body cannot properly digest 'Gluten' is called:
e: Celiac disease is an immune reaction to eating gluten, which damages the lining of the small intestine.

q: Which phase of the cell cycle is the shortest?
e: The actual division phase (M phase) usually lasts only about 1 hour in a typical 24-hour human cell cycle.

q: Which enzyme converts 'Proteoses and Peptones' into Dipeptides in the small intestine?
e: Pancreatic enzymes like trypsin and chymotrypsin continue the protein digestion started in the stomach.

q: The 'Cell Plate' method of cytokinesis occurs in:
e: Plant cells cannot form a cleavage furrow due to the rigid cell wall, so they build a cell plate from the center outward.

q: Which of the following is a function of the 'Large Intestine'?
e: The colon primarily recovers water and electrolytes from the waste material before it is expelled.

q: The 'Bivalents' are formed during which sub-stage of Prophase I?
e: During Zygotene, homologous chromosomes pair up (synapsis) to form bivalents (also called tetrads).

q: What is the role of 'Bile Salts' in digestion?
e: Bile salts break down large fat globules into tiny micelles, increasing the surface area for lipase to work.

q: Which cell organelle is most active during 'Cytokinesis' in plant cells?
e: The Golgi apparatus produces the vesicles that fuse to form the cell plate in plant cell division.

q: Which of the following vitamins is primarily absorbed in the 'Ileum'?
e: Vitamin B12 absorption requires intrinsic factor from the stomach and takes place specifically in the terminal ileum.

q: The division of the nucleus is called ________, while the division of the cytoplasm is called ________.
e: Karyo refers to the nucleus; cyto refers to the cell body (cytoplasm).

q: What is the common passage for the 'Bile duct' and the 'Pancreatic duct'?
e: These two ducts join to form the common hepatopancreatic duct, which opens into the duodenum.

q: The number of chromosomes in a human 'Somatic Cell' is ________, and in a 'Gamete' is ________.
e: Body cells (somatic) are diploid (46 chromosomes), while sex cells (gametes) are haploid (23 chromosomes).

q: Which enzyme converts 'Dipeptides' into 'Amino Acids'?
e: Dipeptidases are part of the intestinal juice (succus entericus) that complete the final step of protein digestion.

q: The stage of the cell cycle where the cell increases in size and replicates its organelles is:
e: G1 is the primary growth phase where the cell increases in mass and duplicates organelles.

q: What is 'Deglutition'?
e: Deglutition is the medical term for the act of swallowing food or liquid.

q: In which stage of meiosis do the sister chromatids finally separate?
e: Anaphase II of meiosis is very similar to Anaphase of mitosis, where the centromeres split and chromatids separate.

q: Which part of the teeth contains the blood vessels and nerves?
e: The pulp cavity is the soft center of the tooth that contains living tissue, providing sensation and nourishment.

q: The term 'Mitosis' was coined by which scientist?
e: Walther Flemming coined the term in 1882 to describe the process of thread-like nuclear division.

q: Which of the following is the inactive precursor of the enzyme 'Pepsin'?
e: Pepsinogen is the zymogen (inactive form) secreted by chief cells. It is converted to active pepsin by stomach acid (HCl).

q: The stage between two meiotic divisions (Meiosis I and Meiosis II) is called:
e: Interkinesis is a short-lived resting stage. Crucially, there is no DNA replication during this phase.

q: Which hormone causes the secretion of bicarbonate-rich pancreatic juice?
e: Secretin is released by the duodenum in response to acid. It stimulates the pancreas to release water and bicarbonate to neutralize the acid.

q: During which phase of Mitosis do the chromosomes reach the poles and begin to decondense?
e: In Telophase, the nuclear envelope reforms around each set of chromosomes as they lose their thread-like appearance.

q: Which of the following is a 'Polysaccharide' that humans cannot digest but acts as roughage?
e: Humans lack the enzyme cellulase. Cellulose stays in the gut and helps in the smooth movement of feces.

q: The pairing of homologous chromosomes is also known as:
e: Synapsis occurs during the Zygotene stage of Meiosis I and is essential for genetic exchange.

q: Which part of the teeth is the most mineralized and hardest part?
e: Tooth enamel is the hardest substance in the human body, containing 96% mineral content.

q: In which stage of Prophase I does the 'Synaptonemal Complex' dissolve?
e: During Diplotene, the paired chromosomes start to separate except at the Chiasmata (crossover points).

q: Which valve prevents the backflow of fecal matter from the large intestine into the small intestine?
e: The ileocaecal valve connects the ileum to the caecum and is a one-way door.

q: The number of Mitotic divisions required to produce 128 cells from a single cell is:
e: Use the formula 2^n. Since 2^7 = 128, it takes 7 rounds of division.

q: Which organelle is considered the 'Origin' of the lysosome?
e: Lysosomes are formed by the budding off of the Golgi complex containing digestive enzymes.

q: What is the primary function of the 'Gallbladder'?
e: Bile is made by the liver. The gallbladder removes water to make the bile more potent for fat digestion.

q: In Meiosis II, what happens during Anaphase II?
e: Anaphase II is like mitosis; the centromeres split and pull the sister chromatids apart.

q: Which cells of the stomach secrete 'Intrinsic Factor' essential for Vitamin B12 absorption?
e: Parietal cells secrete both HCl and Intrinsic Factor. Deficiency leads to pernicious anemia.

q: Which stage of the cell cycle is marked by the 'Replication of Centrioles'?
e: Along with DNA replication in the nucleus, centriole replication occurs in the cytoplasm during the S-phase.

q: What is the 'pH' of the Succus Entericus (Intestinal Juice)?
e: Intestinal juice is slightly alkaline (pH 7.8) to facilitate enzyme activity in the small intestine.

q: Who proposed that 'Cells only arise from pre-existing cells'?
e: Rudolf Virchow added this final tenet to the cell theory (Omnis cellula e cellula).

q: The protein digesting enzyme found in the 'Pancreatic Juice' is:
e: Trypsin, Chymotrypsin, and Carboxypeptidase are the protein enzymes from the pancreas.

q: Which type of chromosome has the centromere located at the very end?
e: Telocentric chromosomes appear 'I-shaped' during anaphase.

q: The yellow pigment 'Bilirubin' is formed from the breakdown of:
e: Old red blood cells are broken down in the spleen and liver, producing bilirubin as a waste product.

q: The smallest cell in the human body is usually considered the:
e: Human sperm cells are significantly smaller than somatic cells or the ovum (the largest cell).

q: Which enzyme converts 'Dipeptides' into individual 'Amino acids'?
e: Dipeptidases are part of the intestinal juice (Succus Entericus) and perform the final step of protein digestion.

q: The number of chromosomes in the human 'Egg cell' is:
e: Gametes (Egg and Sperm) are haploid, meaning they contain only half the chromosome set (23).

q: A 'Balanced Diet' for an adult should typically consist of carbs, proteins, and fats in what ratio?
e: The general recommendation is roughly 4 parts carbohydrates to 1 part protein and 1 part fat.

q: Which cell organelle is called the 'Signal Transducer' of the cell?
e: The cell membrane contains receptors that receive chemical signals and pass the information into the cell.

q: The 'Islets of Langerhans' secrete Insulin from which specific cells?
e: Beta cells produce insulin. Alpha cells produce glucagon.

q: Which stage of mitosis is also known as the 'Disjunction' phase?
e: Anaphase is when sister chromatids physically move apart toward the poles.

q: The 'Dental Formula' for a human child (milk teeth) is:
e: Children lack premolars and the third molar. The formula is 2-1-0-2.

q: Which structure connects the two sister chromatids?
e: The centromere is the constricted region of a chromosome that joins sister chromatids together.

q: The 'Large Intestine' lacks which of the following structures found in the small intestine?
e: Villi are for absorption of nutrients. Since nutrient absorption is mostly finished, the large intestine does not have villi.

q: What is the result of 'Meiosis I'?
e: Meiosis I is the reduction division; it ends with two daughter cells that have half the original chromosomes.

q: Which disorder is characterized by the accumulation of 'Uric Acid' crystals in joints?
e: While not purely digestive, Gout is linked to the diet and metabolic breakdown of purines.

q: Which organelle is responsible for 'Autolysis' (digesting its own cell)?
e: If a cell is damaged, lysosomes rupture and their enzymes digest the whole cell.

q: The common bile duct is formed by the union of:
e: The cystic duct (from gallbladder) and hepatic duct (from liver) join to form the common bile duct.

q: Which of the following describes 'Prokaryotic DNA'?
e: Prokaryotic DNA is found in a nucleoid region, is circular, and lacks histone proteins.

q: Which mineral is required for the 'Coagulation' of blood?
